title: Justicia stellata
intro: Justicia stellata, also known as Starbur or Wild Basil, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia stellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Justicia stellata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Justicia stellata is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is part of the genus Justicia, which consists of approximately 650 species. The genus Justicia is a member of the tribe Justicieae, within the subfamily Acanthoideae. The Acanthaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or acanthus family. The classification of Justicia stellata is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae.
